channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Kids' test scores began declining way before COVID. These schools are making gains
item.description: Remember those devastating learning losses that began during the pandemic? Turns out, they began years before COVID-19. Some states are finally turning things around.
item.content:encoded: Remember those devastating learning losses that began during the pandemic? Turns out, they began years before COVID-19. Some states are finally turning things around.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies at 47 of brain tumor
item.description: Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
Jason Collins, the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league, has died after an eight-month battle with an aggressive form of a brain tumor, his family announced Tuesday.

(Image credit: Mark J. Terrill)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: OpenAI's Sam Altman takes the stand to fend off Elon Musk's accusations he 'stole a charity'
item.description: The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.
item.content:encoded:
The trial pits two of tech's most famous personalities against each other and could lead to seismic changes for the maker of ChatGPT.

(Image credit: JOSH EDELSON)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: The SPLC survived firebombs and death threats. Will it survive Trump 2.0?
item.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center is facing a serious threat from the Justice Department — and comes at a time when several former and current SPLC employees say the group is already deeply vulnerable.
item.content:encoded:
The Southern Poverty Law Center is facing a serious threat from the Justice Department — and comes at a time when several former and current SPLC employees say the group is already deeply vulnerable.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef

item.title: Alabama splits U.S. House primaries after court ruling; S.C. redistricting stalls
item.description: Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the Supreme Court cleared the way — and as redistricting ripples through the South.
item.content:encoded:
Alabama will hold a special primary election for four of its seven congressional districts, after the Supreme Court cleared the way — and as redistricting ripples through the South.

(Image credit: Kim Chandler)
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Swasey

item.title: Transportation Secretary Duffy filmed a reality show, funded by firms he regulates
item.description: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.content:encoded: Secretary Duffy, his wife and nine kids took a "Great American Road Trip" to celebrate America's 250th. They say no taxpayers funded the project. But a nonprofit with transport-related sponsors did.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Nonprofit sues to stop Trump administration from repainting Reflecting Pool blue
item.description: President Trump's Reflecting Pool renovation is coming in wildly over budget. Now, a nonprofit is suing to stop the project, citing the Trump administration's failure to follow procedure.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's Reflecting Pool renovation is coming in wildly over budget. Now, a nonprofit is suing to stop the project, citing the Trump administration's failure to follow procedure.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Drive 5 hours or fly 20 minutes? Remote towns suffer from lack of year-round flights
item.description: A federal program that pays airlines to operate in small and rural communities could have its budget cut in half, leaving parts of the country with no flight options.
item.content:encoded:
A federal program that pays airlines to operate in small and rural communities could have its budget cut in half, leaving parts of the country with no flight options.

(Image credit: Agata Storer for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Sacha Pfeiffer

item.title: 24 hours with 3 teenage birders: Welcome to the World Series of Birding
item.description: The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.
item.content:encoded:
The three high-school birders, dubbed The Pete Dunnelins, have one day to count as many bird species across the state of New Jersey as physically possible. Here's what it takes.

(Image credit: Mohamed Sadek for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Natalie Escobar

item.title: Survey warns that some nonprofits are in danger of closing due to funding cuts
item.description: Nonprofits say they face an existential crisis from funding cuts and other moves by the Trump administration. A new survey warns, some are in danger of folding altogether.
item.content:encoded: Nonprofits say they face an existential crisis from funding cuts and other moves by the Trump administration. A new survey warns, some are in danger of folding altogether.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden
